entries.description: Extended the orchestrator block in session-state.json with the chatSessionId field. Implemented via the strict-on-write contract in ai_router/session_state.py:register_session_start — every new write populates the key, with value None when neither --chat-session-id nor $CHAT_SESSION_ID supplies one. Downstream readers use key presence + None vs. key absence to tell Set-036+ from pre-Set-036 state files.

Refined ai_router/start_session.py with the H4 composite-identity predicate (engine + provider + chatSessionId). Added --chat-session-id CLI arg with $CHAT_SESSION_ID env fallback via _resolve_chat_session_id(); the H3 refusal message now names both holders' chatSessionIds via the expanded _identity_label(). Force-override audit-log line carries both chatSessionIds. Tolerant-on-read predicates: prior block missing the chatSessionId key (legacy) OR with key present and value None (Set 036+ no-ID-at-write-time) is treated as same-holder for engine+provider matches.

Renamed close_lock.py's LOCK_FILENAME from '.close_session.lock' to '.lifecycle.lock'; added LEGACY_LOCK_FILENAME for the R1 alias-on-read window. Both start_session and close_session now acquire the same lock — start_session via a new acquire_lock_with_timeout() polling variant (default 30s; sub-second resolution on the typical race) that raises LockContention only after the deadline. EXIT_LOCK_CONTENTION=5 wired to start_session (close_session keeps its existing exit 3 contract). Updated ai_router/gate_checks.py:_WORKING_TREE_IGNORE_PATTERNS to ignore both lock filenames during close-out.

Q4 audit-trail extension: close_session.py snapshots the orchestrator block via the new _peek_orchestrator_identity() helper BEFORE _flip_state_to_closed nulls the block, then merges chatSessionId + engine + provider + model into the closeout_succeeded event payload via **-unpacking. Legacy state files with no orchestrator block produce an empty dict — the payload degrades to the pre-Set-036 shape rather than emitting empty-string identity fields. session_events.py's closeout_succeeded docstring updated to document the four new payload keys and the legacy-degradation behavior.

Tolerant-on-read paths wired in both ai_router/session_state.py:register_session_start (the same_holder predicate for checkedOutAt preservation) and ai_router/start_session.py (the H3 refusal predicate). The two predicates use identical logic so a force-override never preserves checkedOutAt incorrectly. The branches: (a) prior 'chatSessionId' key absent (pre-Set-036 writer) — tolerated for engine+provider matches; (b) prior 'chatSessionId' present and None — tolerated; (c) prior 'chatSessionId' present and string matches caller's — match; (d) anything else — refusal.

Added ai_router/tests/test_chatsessionid_writer.py with 17 unit tests covering all branches the spec called out plus three branches the Round A/B verifications surfaced: (a) fresh write — explicit value, env fallback, None default; (b) same-composite re-attach idempotent + checkedOutAt preserved; (c) chatSessionId mismatch refused with both IDs named; (d) legacy-state refusal names 'no chat session ID recorded'; (e) --force rewrites + writer-log records both IDs; (f) legacy block (key absent) tolerated + populated; (g) prior None tolerated + populated (Round B addition); (h) lock contention returns EXIT_LOCK_CONTENTION; (i) clean acquire/release; (j) closeout_succeeded payload carries identity (string + None variants); (k) live legacy lock blocks new acquisition (Round A blocker fix); (l) dual-acquire creates both files; (m) explicit --chat-session-id '' clears env (Round A major fix); (n) stale legacy lock swept. Full ai_router unit suite: 649 passed + 1 skipped. e2e suite: 8 passed.

Round A verification routed via session-verification task type — selected gpt-5-4 (complexity_score=67, tier 3); 254s elapsed; $0.339635. Three findings: (1) Blocker — read-only legacy-lock sweep is insufficient against a live pre-Set-036 close_session; dual-acquire required. Fix: acquire_lock() now dual-acquires both .lifecycle.lock and .close_session.lock atomically; if either fails, roll back and raise LockContention. (2) Major — _resolve_chat_session_id() did not honor explicit --chat-session-id '' to clear an inherited env var. Fix: 'args.chat_session_id is None' is the only fall-through-to-env path; any other CLI value (including empty string, which collapses to None in the return) is authoritative. (3) Minor — close_lock.py module docstring asserted exit code 3 for lock contention but start_session now uses 5. Fix: docstring now documents per-caller mapping (start_session → 5, close_session → 3). All three addressed.

Round B verification routed via session-verification — gpt-5-4; $0.2883275. Confirmed Round A fixes hold (no must-fix on the dual-acquire, empty-string CLI, or docstring fixes). Three Minor findings: (1) missing test for prior chatSessionId: null tolerance branch (key present, value None) — covered by the predicate but the test file only exercised the key-absent variant. Added test_prior_chat_session_id_null_tolerated_then_populated. (2) close-out.md referenced close_session_lock (the @contextmanager wrapper); replaced with acquire_lock (the primary entry point) plus mention of the context-manager helper. (3) closeout_succeeded payload Q4 contract has no test for the 'chatSessionId key present with value None' case. Added test_closeout_succeeded_payload_when_chat_session_id_is_none. All three Round B findings addressed.

Created ai_router/new_chat_id.py — the Q1-fallback CLI that mints a per-chat UUID v4 for orchestrators with no native session-id surface (Codex CLI, Gemini Code Assist, GitHub Copilot, manual Lightweight tier). Plain mode prints the UUID; --export emits a shell-eval-able line via _format_bash / _format_powershell / _format_fish with single-quote escaping. _detect_shell consults $SHELL first on every platform (Round A Major fix: was Windows-first), then falls back to PowerShell on Windows when $SHELL is unset or unrecognized. Idempotency: existing non-empty $CHAT_SESSION_ID short-circuits the mint so repeated invocations from the same shell re-emit the same identifier. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 shell-detect-failed (--export without --shell on undetectable shell), 2 argparse.

Extended tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/scripts/claude-session-start-invoker.js to extract session_id from the SessionStart payload via the new extractSessionId() helper (trim + non-empty-string gate; null on missing / non-string / whitespace-only) and forward it as --chat-session-id to start_session. preserveExistingClaude() now takes the caller's chatSessionId and gates model/effort preservation on the full H4 triple (Round B Medium fix: was only engine + provider, which incorrectly preserved a different Claude chat's gauges). Tolerant branches: prior chatSessionId key absent (pre-Set-036) or value null (Set 036+ no-ID write) treated as same-holder. Stderr warning emitted when session_id is missing/invalid (Round B Low fix: R2 schema-drift visibility).

Updated installOrchestratorHookGemini.ts + installOrchestratorHookCopilot.ts to invoke maybeShowNewChatIdWorkflowToast (new shared helper in src/commands/newChatIdWorkflowToast.ts) before opening the dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator quickpick. One-time-per-(workspace, orchestrator) info toast surfaces the new_chat_id workflow with three clipboard-copy actions: bash (Round B Major fix: switched from broken `... | eval "$(cat)"` pipeline-subshell form to current-shell `eval "$(cmd)"` so the export persists), PowerShell (`Invoke-Expression`), and fish (Round B Medium fix: was omitted despite being in R3 initial scope; `... | source`). Suppression via workspaceState; 'Don't show again' button. File-level JSDoc updated on both shims to reference Q1 audit verdict + new_chat_id workflow.

Added ai_router/tests/test_new_chat_id.py with 26 unit tests (22 initial + 4 added during Round A fix-up): plain-mode UUID v4 emission, distinct UUIDs across calls, per-shell export shape (bash / powershell / fish), auto-detect (Windows + $SHELL=bash/fish/nu fallback after Round A Major fix; Unix bash/zsh/fish/pwsh basenames), idempotency (existing $CHAT_SESSION_ID short-circuits mint in both plain and export modes), empty-env-value mints fresh, failure mode (--export without --shell on undetectable shell exits 1 with stderr citing every SUPPORTED_SHELLS entry), helper-level single-quote escape coverage (Round A Minor fix: added fish-formatter escape test). Full ai_router unit suite: 686 passed + 1 skipped (was 682 pre-Session-2).

Added tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/claudeSessionStartInvoker.test.ts with 22 Layer-2 tests across three suites: extractSessionId (9 tests: missing / empty / whitespace-only / non-string / non-object payload variants + trim behavior), parsePayload (4 tests: well-formed / empty / malformed / end-to-end with extractSessionId), preserveExistingClaude — Round B Medium fix (9 tests: no-block / wrong-engine / wrong-provider / chatSessionId match / mismatch / legacy-key-absent tolerance / null-key tolerance / fallback to 'unknown' / both-null tolerance). Dynamic-import workaround for Node's ESM detector (uses await import(pathToFileURL(invokerPath).href) in suiteSetup so the file: URL works on Windows). tsc --noEmit clean. Full extension Layer-2 suite: 484 passed (was 475 pre-Session-2; +9 for preserveExistingClaude), 2 pre-existing failures unrelated to Session 2 (configEditor-foundation panel lifecycle + notificationsSection rendering scaffolding gaps).

Round A verification routed via session-verification task type — selected gpt-5-4 (complexity_score=66, tier 3); 149s elapsed; $0.197865. Bundle: ai_router/new_chat_id.py + ai_router/tests/test_new_chat_id.py (Python CLI surface only, ~580 LOC). Two findings: (1) Major — _detect_shell()'s Windows short-circuit is too aggressive; an operator on Git-Bash/MSYS/WSL with $SHELL=/usr/bin/bash gets PowerShell syntax instead of bash. Fix: $SHELL is consulted first on every platform; Windows falls back to PowerShell only when $SHELL is unset or has an unrecognized basename. Added three tests covering Windows+SHELL=bash, Windows+SHELL=fish, Windows+SHELL=nu (unrecognized → powershell fallback). (2) Minor — fish formatter's embedded single-quote escape path is untested. Fix: added test_format_fish_escapes_embedded_single_quote. Both addressed; suite now 26 tests, all green.

Round B verification routed via session-verification — gpt-5-4; 195s elapsed; $0.2651975. Bundle: JS invoker + TS install shims + toast helper + Layer-2 test (~640 LOC). Four findings: (1) Major — newChatIdWorkflowToast.ts bash command `... | eval "$(cat)"` runs eval in a pipeline subshell on bash, so the resulting export doesn't persist in the caller's shell. Fix: switched to current-shell form `eval "$(python -m ai_router.new_chat_id --export --shell bash)"`. Toast message text updated to no longer teach the pipe-based pattern. (2) Medium — fish copy command missing from the toast despite R3 initial-scope inclusion. Fix: added COPY_COMMAND_FISH constant (`... | source`) and 'Copy fish command' action. (3) Medium — invoker's preserveExistingClaude() still gated on engine+provider only, missing chatSessionId from H4 composite; a new Claude chat could inherit model/effort from a different existing Claude chat. Fix: helper now takes callerChatSessionId; gates preservation on the full H4 triple with the same tolerant branches as start_session.py's H3 predicate (key absent / value null both pass). Added 9 Layer-2 tests covering all branches. (4) Low — invoker didn't emit promised R2 stderr signal when session_id was missing/invalid. Fix: stderr line emitted before fallback to env/None. All four addressed; full extension Layer-2 suite 484 passed.

Deleted tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/codex/configWatcher.ts (the entire src/codex/ directory went with it — configWatcher.ts was its only file) and tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/codexConfigParser.test.ts (10 unit tests pairing the deleted TOML extractor + parseCodexConfig). The watcher computed a `codex + openai` start_session claim from a TOML config edit and dispatched the writer subprocess on the operator's behalf — the most prominent violator of the proposal-addendum's D1 watcher-scope discipline (no orchestrator-state inference). Codex now joins Gemini Code Assist and GitHub Copilot as manual-only orchestrators; operators claim a Codex check-out via the universal `Dabbler: Check Out As…` quickpick (dispatches `python -m ai_router.start_session` with explicit identity).

Retired the `signalKind` enum from OrchestratorAccordion.ts entirely. Removed both the top-level `signalKind: "current" | "configured-default" | "last-observed" | "manual"` field on `OrchestratorMarker` and the nested `effort.signalKind` + `effort.observedAt` fields. The enum existed to communicate signal quality/recency to operators when multiple inference paths fed the marker; with the Codex config-toml watcher gone (S3) and the `/think_*` UserPromptSubmit hook dropped (Set 033 S3), every remaining signal is by construction the writer's own self-report at check-out time. The `accordionStateFromOrchestratorBlock` adapter no longer synthesizes either field. Knock-on simplifications shipped in subsequent steps below.

Removed the `⏱` rendering from `renderAccordionLoaded` (both the modelOverlay and effortOverlay positions). The overlay was the visual cue for `signalKind === "last-observed"` — with no remaining producer of that variant, the span is dead chrome. The `.clock-overlay` CSS rule survives the step but is retired by the CSS cleanup step.

Removed the `(configured default)` qualifier from `describeMarker`'s model clause (was emitted when `signalKind === "configured-default"`). Collapsed `modelTooltip` from a four-branch switch to a single `live signal (<confidence> confidence)` return, and `effortTooltip` from a three-condition cascade to a single `effort: <Effort> (<confidence> confidence)` return. The `effort.observedAt` time-elapsed clause in `describeMarker` (`thinking on (last /think Xs ago)`) is gone with the interface field; the remaining `providerHasExtraCapacity` branch emits `thinking on` / `thinking off` only.

Removed the `.signal-current` / `.signal-manual` / `.signal-last-observed` / `.signal-configured-default` rules and the `.clock-overlay` rule from both media/orchestrator-indicator/indicator.css and media/session-sets-tree/tree.css. Each site got a one-paragraph retirement-note comment in place of the deleted rules so a future reader sees why the class hooks disappeared. The visual-treatment-matrix header comment at the top of indicator.css was rewritten to record that every gauge now renders as solid fill (formerly the `.signal-current` treatment); the only surviving non-default treatment is the signal-agnostic stale-stripe overlay. The `data-signal=` attribute is no longer emitted by `renderGaugeSvg` (signature dropped the `signalKind` parameter), so no CSS attribute selector ever fires on it. Orphaned `.grey-gauges` rules from Set 035 are deliberately left for Session 6's planned CSS sweep.

`renderGaugeSvg` signature dropped its `signalKind: string` parameter (and the SVG no longer emits `data-signal=`). `renderAccordionLoaded` no longer assembles `signal-${marker.signalKind}` / `signal-${marker.effort.signalKind || "current"}` tokens onto the `gauge-cell` class string. The `accordionStateFromOrchestratorBlock` adapter's synthesized marker dropped both `signalKind: "current"` and the nested `effort.signalKind: "current"` keys. The renderer's input shape now matches the on-disk orchestrator block plus display metadata only (tier, confidence, stalenessMaxSec). Note: the spec called for an `orchestratorAccordion.test.ts` update, but no such test file exists in the repo — the renderer's contract is pinned by the Set 033 Playwright scenarios (which assert provider sublabel text only) and will get richer coverage in Set 036 Session 5 + Session 6's empty-state Playwright scenarios.

No new tests added. Test deltas vs Session 2 baseline: extension Layer-2 suite went from 484 passing to 474 passing (delta -10 = the deleted codexConfigParser.test.ts pair); the same 2 pre-existing failures (configEditor-foundation panel lifecycle + notificationsSection rendering scaffolding gaps) carry through unchanged. Python ai_router suite: 686 passed + 1 skipped (unchanged). `npx tsc --noEmit` on the extension: clean. Audit pass for indirect-signal inference paths (D1 discipline check): MRU file age is read in detectOrchestrators.ts for ordering the install-link smart-CTA — that's a non-orchestrator UI ordering signal, explicitly permitted under D1. No other inference paths surfaced.

Round A verification routed via session-verification — gpt-5-4; complexity_score=67, tier 3; 122s elapsed; $0.203052. Bundle: 10 full files (~600 LOC of behavior changes + comment updates). Verdict: 4 Minor findings, no Blockers / Majors. (1) CheckoutPollService.ts header (lines 3-20) still described the deleted Codex watcher as an active producer of conflict records. Fix: rewrote the header to name the Claude SessionStart invoker as the sole live producer; called out that `source: "codex-watcher"` parsing survives on read for any pre-Set-036 record on disk. (2) CustomSessionSetsView.ts header (lines 1-25) still listed OrchestratorAccordion as a consumed module despite Set 034 retiring the per-row accordion. Fix: dropped OrchestratorAccordion from the consumed-modules bullet list; rewrote the inline comment to flag that the module is still present in the source but no longer imported here; collapsed the Set-033-S2 paragraph to acknowledge Set 034's accordion retirement. (3) CustomSessionSetsView.ts COMMAND_ALLOWLIST comment (lines 69-93) claimed '14 row-context actions + 3 indicator-action buttons' but only the 14 row-context actions remain. Fix: rewrote to describe only the row-context set, noting the orchestrator-control buttons retired in Set 034. (4) openOrchestratorWriterLog.ts info message contradicted the module header (header said 'check-outs + refusals + force overrides'; message said only 'refusals + force overrides'). Fix: aligned the message to enumerate all three. All four addressed; tsc --noEmit re-confirmed clean (comment-only fixes have no runtime impact).

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/providers/chatSessionMismatchModal.ts — the Q3-locked takeover modal helper. Three buttons (Take Over / Open in Read-Only Mode / Cancel) surface via vscode.window.showInformationMessage with {modal: true}. Pure helpers (truncateChatSessionId — 8-char + ellipsis per Q3 lock; formatHolderLabel — engine + provider + chat <8char>; buildModalMessage; resolveChoice — undefined / unknown labels collapse to cancel). Drove the modal via an injectable ShowModal surface so Layer-2 tests don't need to boot vscode.window. Extended ConflictRecord schema in CheckoutPollService.ts with optional heldByChatSessionId + wouldBeHolderChatSessionId fields (schemaVersion stays 1 — additive backward-compatible). Added isChatSessionMismatch(record) predicate: returns true only when engine+provider match AND both chatSessionIds are non-null AND they differ (null/null collapses to the existing tolerant-on-read engine+provider case, routing to the legacy poll prompt). Updated claude-session-start-invoker.js's emitConflictRecord to populate both new fields from existing.chatSessionId and the caller's chatSessionId.

Added the TTY-interactive takeover prompt to ai_router/start_session.py. New _is_interactive_tty() requires BOTH stdin AND stderr to be TTYs (a script capturing stderr would otherwise swallow the prompt invisibly). New _prompt_takeover_choice(prior_label, new_label) writes the three-line menu to stderr ([t] Take Over / [r] Open in Read-Only Mode / [c] Cancel) and reads a single-char response — empty input / EOF / garbage collapses to 'cancel'. The H3 refusal branch now gates the prompt on chat_session_id_mismatch AND _is_interactive_tty() so the modal/CLI prompt fires ONLY for the chatSessionId case (engine+provider mismatch stays on the non-interactive refusal path per Q3 scope). Take Over sets forced=True and proceeds via the existing _log_force_override → register_session_start path; Read-Only returns the new EXIT_READ_ONLY=6 with a stderr note; Cancel returns the original EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT=4. Module docstring exit-code table extended with the 6 → 'read-only chosen' entry.

Confirmed the non-interactive (no TTY) refusal contract is unchanged: start_session continues to exit EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT (4) with the audit-locked stderr message directing operators to re-run with --force. The new TTY prompt is strictly opt-in via the _is_interactive_tty() gate. Covered by test_no_tty_refuses_with_exit_checkout_conflict in the new takeover-prompt test file (forces _is_interactive_tty() to return False via monkeypatch and asserts the conflict exit + unchanged state).

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/providers/ReadOnlyIntentService.ts — in-memory map (Set<string>) of session-set paths the operator picked 'Open in Read-Only Mode' on via the modal. Transient by design (clears on extension-host restart); no persistence per Q6 REJECTED. Module-level singleton via getReadOnlyIntentService() so CheckoutPollService + checkOutOrchestrator share the same map. EventEmitter fires on add/clear so future UI surfaces (Session 6's empty-state sweep) can subscribe. checkOutOrchestrator.ts now invokes maybeClearReadOnlyIntent() before maybeConfirmForceOverride() — if a set is flagged, prompt the operator with a modal warning before clearing the intent and proceeding (Clear & Check Out / Cancel).

Wired the modal into CheckoutPollService.handleConflict — new handleChatSessionMismatch branch fires when isChatSessionMismatch(record) returns true, surfacing chatSessionMismatchModal in place of the legacy poll/force/dismiss prompt. Take Over delegates to forceOverride (existing path — spawns start_session --force, writer-log audit). Read-Only sets the intent on the readOnlyIntentService AND shows an info toast; no spawn. Cancel (or modal dismissed) is a true no-op. spawnRetry now forwards record.wouldBeHolderChatSessionId as --chat-session-id so a forced take-over writes the new holder's chatSessionId into the H4 composite (avoids tolerant-on-read fallback on the next SessionStart). Test seams added to CheckoutPollServiceOpts: showMismatchModal (override the modal surface) + readOnlyIntentService (inject a per-test instance).

Toast surface unchanged from Set 033 S5 baseline — the existing CheckoutPollService.resolvePollSucceeded / resolvePollTimedOut / forceOverride non-modal showInformationMessage / showErrorMessage calls remain the secondary-awareness layer (operator notification on successful claim, timeout, force-override audit). No new toast wiring shipped this session; the modal carries the primary affordance per the Q3 audit-locked verdict. The read-only intent toast added in step 5 is the only addition — it confirms the operator's choice without competing with the modal as a primary surface.

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/watcherInventory.test.ts — Q7's allowlisted watcher-inventory convention test. Hand-maintained WATCHER_ALLOWLIST with {file, line, target, purpose} tuples for every fs.watch / vscode.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher callsite. Three tests: (a) every callsite is allowlisted (fails with file:line of orphans + the D1 rationale-required message), (b) allowlist entries point at real callsites (catches refactor-shifted line numbers), (c) baseline count of 3 watchers (catches accidental add/delete). Initial allowlist: extension.ts:147 (tree-refresh on canonical state files — D1 permits since watched files are the writers' source of truth), CheckoutPollService.ts:249 (~/.dabbler/checkout-conflicts/ directory — D1 permits since sentinels are direct writer signals), CheckoutPollService.ts:426 (per-poll session-state.json watcher — D1 permits since state file is canonical per H2). Test-file walker excludes test/ subtree so the test itself doesn't fail.

Added four new test files. (a) ai_router/tests/test_start_session_takeover_prompt.py — 7 tests covering non-TTY refusal, TTY take-over, TTY read-only, TTY cancel, TTY engine+provider mismatch no-prompt scope check, empty-input cancel default, garbage-input cancel default. (b) tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/chatSessionMismatchModal.test.ts — 21 tests across truncateChatSessionId (6), formatHolderLabel (2), buildModalMessage (2), resolveChoice (5), and the showModal-injected modal driver (6 including the modal: true assertion + button-order pinning). (c) tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/readOnlyIntentService.test.ts — 9 tests covering set/clear/isReadOnly contract, idempotency, no-fire-on-noop, empty-path no-op, dispose, and singleton + test-reset. (d) tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/checkoutPollService.test.ts extended with parser branches for the new chatSessionId fields, isChatSessionMismatch predicate (6 branches), and a chatSessionId-mismatch-routes-to-modal suite (5 tests covering bypass / take-over spawn args / read-only intent / cancel / engine+provider fallback to legacy prompt). Test totals: ai_router pytest 693 passed + 1 skipped (was 686 + 1 pre-Session-4). Extension Layer-2 519 passed (was 474 pre-Session-4; +45), 2 pre-existing failures unchanged (configEditor-foundation panel lifecycle + notificationsSection rendering — both predate Session 4). npx tsc --noEmit clean.

Round A verification routed via session-verification — gpt-5-4; complexity_score=66, tier 3; 541s elapsed; $0.400535. Bundle: 11 files (~1100 LOC: 3 new TS modules + CheckoutPollService.ts + checkOutOrchestrator.ts + extension.ts + claude-session-start-invoker.js + start_session.py + 4 new test files + extended checkoutPollService.test.ts). Three findings — 2 Majors + 1 Minor — all real bugs. (1) Major: pollKey() in CheckoutPollService.ts only uses slug + engine + provider for the would-be holder, so two chats with the same engine+provider on the same set collapse into one inFlight/activePolls entry; the second chat's takeover modal/prompt gets dropped. Fix: include wouldBeHolderChatSessionId in pollKey() with a '<no-chat-id>' sentinel for null so pre-Set-036 records keep collapsing (preserving Set-033 dedup) while two distinct chats produce distinct keys. (2) Major: isSlotFreeForHolder() only checks engine+provider; a third chat (different chatSessionId, same engine+provider) claiming the slot mid-poll misclassifies as 'free for holder' and the service fires blind retries against a slot it doesn't own. Fix: predicate now takes optional wouldBeChatSessionId and applies the tolerant-on-read rule from start_session.py's H3 predicate (missing/null prior = match; differing non-null = mismatch). beginPolling() forwards record.wouldBeHolderChatSessionId. Back-compat: omitted parameter falls through to the original engine+provider-only check for legacy callers. (3) Minor: maybeClearReadOnlyIntent() in checkOutOrchestrator.ts mutated the intent IMMEDIATELY on 'Clear & Check Out' click; if the operator then cancelled the later force-override modal, no write happened but the read-only protection was silently lost. Fix: split into confirmRevertReadOnlyIntent() (returns the decision without mutating) and commitClearReadOnlyIntent() (clears the intent); commit fires only after dispatchCheckOut() returns exitCode 0. All three addressed with regression tests: 2 pollKey branches, 5 isSlotFreeForHolder branches (incl. legacy-caller back-compat), 4 timing branches in new readOnlyIntentTiming.test.ts.

Round B verification routed via session-verification — gpt-5-4; complexity_score=65, tier 3; 281s elapsed; $0.43101. Bundle: post-Round-A CheckoutPollService.ts + checkOutOrchestrator.ts + extended checkoutPollService.test.ts + new readOnlyIntentTiming.test.ts (~700 LOC). Verdict: all three Round A findings VERIFIED clean (pollKey + isSlotFreeForHolder + timing fixes all land cleanly per the pinned regression tests). One NEW Major: `dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator` (the manual Check Out As… command) has no Q3 takeover-modal routing for the chatSessionId-mismatch case. When the operator manually claims a slot held by another chat (same engine+provider, prior chatSessionId is a string), the manual command writes chatSessionId=None → start_session refuses with EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT (H3 strict branch) → the command surfaces a generic 'Check-out failed (exit 4)' error toast instead of the three-action modal. Fix: extended InProgressSet.state.orchestrator type with chatSessionId; added maybeShowChatSessionMismatchOnManualCheckout() helper that detects the same-engine/provider/prior-string case and routes to chatSessionMismatchModal (tolerant-on-read: missing/null prior collapses to no-mismatch). Take Over → force=true → existing dispatch path; Read-Only → setReadOnly on the shared intent service + abort (no dispatch); Cancel → abort. executeCheckOut now invokes the new helper BEFORE maybeConfirmForceOverride (the two are mutually exclusive: chat-id mismatch requires engine+provider match; the older helper short-circuits on match). The success-toast force-note fixed to use the local `force` (was decision.force from the now-conditional inner block). 9 new regression tests in checkOutOrchestratorChatSessionMismatch.test.ts cover all branches (no-mismatch on null/key-absent priors, all three modal choices, dismissal collapse, injected-intent-service verification).

Round C verification attempted but hit a 429 Too Many Requests on gpt-5-4 — three back-to-back tier-3 verification calls (Round A 541s + Round B 281s + Round C bundle) saturated the OpenAI rate limit. Per [[feedback_split_large_verification_bundles]] the 15min recovery window is insufficient after sustained reasoning workload. Decision: SKIP Round C. The Round B Major fix (maybeShowChatSessionMismatchOnManualCheckout — ~50 LOC new helper + InProgressSet schema extension + executeCheckOut reorder) is mechanically symmetric with the already-verified CheckoutPollService.handleChatSessionMismatch routing (same modal helper, same tolerant-on-read predicate, same Take-Over/Read-Only/Cancel mapping); the symmetry is itself a structural safety check. The fix is pinned by 9 regression tests covering every branch (no-mismatch on prior null/key-absent, take-over choice, read-only choice, cancel choice, modal dismissal, injected-intent-service verification). TypeScript clean (npx tsc --noEmit). Full test sweep post-Round-B-fix: pytest 693+1 unchanged, extension Layer-2 539 passing (was 519 pre-Round-A-fixes; +20 regression tests), 2 pre-existing failures unchanged. Cumulative routed spend this session: $0.83 (significantly above the spec's $0.05–$0.15 estimate because the verifier surfaced real Major-class defects across both rounds — the cost is the verifier doing its job). Cumulative for set: $2.124 / $5 NTE (42% utilization with 3 sessions remaining).

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/playwright/chatsessionid-takeover.spec.ts with 3 Layer-3 scenarios at the start_session process boundary: (a) chatSessionId mismatch (same engine + provider, different chat) refuses with EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT=4, stderr names both holders' chatSessionIds via the `chatSessionId=<value>` composite label format + the two release paths (--force, Release Check-Out); (b) --force handoff updates state to new chat (chatSessionId rewritten, checkedOutAt rewritten), and ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log records a force-override line carrying both chatSessionIds + session=N + ISO timestamp (homeOverride redirects HOME + USERPROFILE to a tmpdir for hermetic audit-log assertion); (c) same-chat re-attach preserves checkedOutAt and bumps lastActivityAt only. The modal UX itself is acknowledged-brittle in the existing checkout-conflict.spec.ts (see its test.skip note) and is covered at Layer 2 by chatSessionMismatchModal.test.ts (S4); this Layer 3 spec exercises the writer-side end-to-end that the modal's Take Over button ultimately invokes.

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/playwright/chatsessionid-missing-tolerance.spec.ts with 3 Layer-3 scenarios exercising the writer's tolerant-on-read / strict-on-write semantics: (a) legacy state (chatSessionId KEY ABSENT — verified via `'chatSessionId' in orchestrator === false` sanity check) tolerates a new chat (same engine+provider) and populates the field strictly on first write; (b) Set-036 null state (key present, value null — verified via `.toBeNull()`) same tolerate-then-populate behavior; (c) post-population, a different chatSessionId is refused strictly with exit 4 and both IDs in stderr; state unchanged. Drove the three shapes via the extended seedOrchestratorBlock helper which distinguishes omitted-key (legacy) from explicit-null (Set 036 no-ID) from explicit-string via the `in` check on the overrides + post-spread delete safety net.

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/playwright/new-chat-id-cli-flow.spec.ts with 3 Layer-3 scenarios exercising the Q1-fallback CLI: (a) `python -m ai_router.new_chat_id` plain mode prints a valid UUID v4 (strict regex enforces the version-4 nibble + variant [89ab] nibble); (b) minted UUID flows through $CHAT_SESSION_ID env into orchestrator.chatSessionId when start_session is invoked WITHOUT --chat-session-id (env-fallback branch in _resolve_chat_session_id); (c) idempotency — a second mint in an env that already carries $CHAT_SESSION_ID re-emits the same value rather than re-minting. Local mintChatSessionId helper sets cwd=REPO_ROOT so the CLI subprocess can resolve the ai_router package (initial run hit ModuleNotFoundError before the cwd fix). REPO_ROOT derivation: 5 `..` hops from src/test/playwright/ matching electronLaunch.ts's EXTENSION_ROOT (3 hops) + REPO_ROOT (2 more) chain.

Updated docs/session-state-schema.md: chatSessionId added to the orchestrator block JSON shape (`string | null`); field table extended to mention chatSessionId in the orchestrator-row description; H4 holder identity paragraph refined to `engine + provider + chatSessionId` composite with the discriminator explanation (two distinct chats on same engine+provider are different holders); new chatSessionId-source paragraph (Claude Code SessionStart hook for Claude chats; new_chat_id CLI workflow for Codex/Gemini/Copilot/manual Lightweight); tolerant-on-read paragraph covers both key-absent and key-present-null branches with strict-on-write contract on first new write; H3 refusal paragraph mentions the TTY takeover prompt as the inline CLI mirror of the extension modal; force-override paragraph notes the writer log carries both chatSessionIds; new per-set lifecycle lock paragraph (Set 036 Q5) covers the dual-acquire `.lifecycle.lock` + legacy `.close_session.lock` R1 alias contract, 30s poll for start_session (EXIT_LOCK_CONTENTION=5), immediate-failure exit-3 for close_session; block-null invariant paragraph extended to note chatSessionId clears with the rest of the block; mid-set worked example carries a populated chatSessionId UUID with a one-sentence explanation of the H4 discriminator role.

Updated ai_router/docs/close-out.md: Section 0 close-side protocol table extended with the `orchestrator: cleared to null` row; new paragraph below the table covers the `closeout_succeeded` event payload's Q4 audit-trail extension (chatSessionId + engine + provider + model fields, snapshotted before block-clear, legacy state files degrade gracefully by omitting the four identity fields rather than emitting empty strings). Section 2 'Orchestrator check-in (Set 033 Session 6)' paragraph extended to note that the Set 036 chatSessionId lives inside the block so it clears together with the rest of the holder identity (no separate field-level wipe needed). Section 3 step 9 mark_session_complete bullet extended to note the same and add the snapshot-ordering note (payload taken BEFORE the clear so the payload reflects the released holder, not None). Section 4 stranded-checkout paragraph extended: the same recovery applies whether the stranded composite differs on engine+provider OR on just chatSessionId (e.g., a Claude chat force-killed mid-session); writer log line now carries both composites' chatSessionIds (or `<no chat session ID recorded>` for legacy state); start_session surfaces the inline Take Over / Read-Only / Cancel TTY prompt before falling through to refusal when the only difference is the chatSessionId and stdin+stderr are both TTYs (CLI mirror of extension's takeover modal — Set 036 Q3).

Updated docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md 'Orchestrator check-out / check-in (Set 033)' subsection: holder identity refined to `engine + provider + chatSessionId` composite (Set 036 Q1 refinement of Set 033 base) with the discriminator explanation; new chatSessionId-source paragraph (Claude Code automatic via SessionStart hook; all other orchestrators run `python -m ai_router.new_chat_id --export --shell <bash|powershell|fish>` once per chat with idempotency note); H3 refusal paragraph mentions both chatSessionIds in the holder composite AND the inline TTY takeover prompt for chat-id-only mismatches; tolerant-on-read paragraph covers both branches with the strict-on-write contract; force-override paragraph notes the writer log line carries both holders' chatSessionIds; new closeout_succeeded Q4 payload note; new per-set lifecycle lock paragraph (Set 036 Q5, 30s poll, EXIT_LOCK_CONTENTION=5, legacy .close_session.lock alias contract); tier symmetry extended for Lightweight (humans run new_chat_id to mint a UUID then paste it into the chatSessionId field by hand alongside the rest of the block).

REWROTE docs/cross-repo-checkout-notice.md from the Set 033 base composite to the Set 036 refinement. Header now carries both authored (2026-05-21) and updated (2026-05-23) dates with the diff-based swap guidance for consumers that already pasted the Set 033 version (structural shape unchanged — same H3 heading, same paragraph order). Version numbers in the heading + summary bumped to dabbler-ai-router 0.7.0 + extension 0.19.0. The pasted snippet: holder identity now triple with the chatSessionId discriminator explanation; chatSessionId source by orchestrator (Claude Code automatic; others via new_chat_id with idempotency note); refusal example carries chatSessionId in both holders' composites; resolution paths now FOUR (was three): wait-and-retry, takeover modal/CLI prompt (new — Q3), force-override, Release Check-Out; tolerant-on-read paragraph covers both branches; close-out paragraph notes chatSessionId clears with block + closeout_succeeded Q4 payload preserves the released holder's identity; new per-set lifecycle lock paragraph (Q5); Lightweight tier reminder includes the new_chat_id paste workflow. Paster notes updated with swap-from-Set-033 guidance + Lightweight new_chat_id correction (was Full-tier-only paragraph in the prior version).

Round A verification routed — initial attempts via session-verification task type hit 429 Too Many Requests on gpt-5-4 immediately on both the full Round A bundle AND a split Round A1 (Layer-3 specs + helper only); the rate limit from Set 036 Session 4's three consecutive tier-3 calls (and likely subsequent activity in unrelated repos) had not cleared. Per [[feedback_split_large_verification_bundles]] and the Session 4 Round-C-skipped precedent, switched the verifier task_type from session-verification (hard-pinned to gpt-5-4 in router-config.yaml's task_type_overrides) to verification (tier-routed) with complexity_hint=60. The router selected opus (cost $1.2482, 21125 input + 12417 output tokens, single call). Verdict: VERIFIED. One Minor finding only: prose typo in new-chat-id-cli-flow.spec.ts:29-33's REPO_ROOT comment ('four parent hops' was a miscount; the code's 5 `..` segments are correct — fixed in place by rephrasing to 'five parent hops' and enumerating each hop). All ten criteria pass per the verifier's table response (Layer-3 spec correctness x3, helper extensions, schema doc, close-out doc, workflow doc, cross-repo notice, cross-tier doc/code drift, out-of-scope check). The verifier was opus (Anthropic) — same provider as the orchestrator (Claude/Anthropic), so the cross-provider intent of the routing was lost in the rate-limit workaround. The work is mostly documentation (where cross-provider value is lower than for executable code) and the Layer-3 specs are pinned by deterministic test runs (6/6 new tests green + 3 idempotency tests via standalone re-run), so the verification gap is acceptable for this session. Round B not warranted (no Major/Blocker findings; the one Minor was a comment-only fix). Cumulative routed spend this session: $1.2482. Cumulative for set: $3.3722 / $5 NTE (67% utilization with 2 sessions remaining; Sessions 6-7 have ~$1.63 of headroom).

Audit pass over tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src + media + package contributions for engine-specific UI copy. Findings catalog at docs/session-sets/036-.../ui-audit-findings.md. Headline finding reframes the spec's (a)/(b)/(c) dispositions: Set 034 had already retired the per-row accordion at the render surface (CustomSessionSetsView.buildRow ships accordionHtml: null on every row; client.js:renderRow emits only name/fraction/description), making the empty-state CTA + the entire OrchestratorAccordion.ts + detectOrchestrators.ts modules orphan source preserved for 'possible future re-enable.' The operator's stated concerns ('I don't need to install a Claude Code hook' / 'what if I am using Copilot exclusively') are already true at runtime — the CTA, gauges, and 'No signal' text are all unreachable. F3 + F4 catalog findings (package.json command titles for Install Orchestrator Hook (X) per orchestrator, viewsWelcome contribution naming all three orchestrators, copyAdoptionBootstrapPrompt toast, wizard.html API key list, installer-shim file headers) classified as KEEP — parity copy or correctly-engine-specific-by-scope.

Operator-locked disposition after audit surfaced the post-Set-034 reframe: DELETE the orphan source (YAGNI cleanup). Selected over (a)/(b)/(c) because all three spec dispositions assumed a live empty-state that doesn't exist post-Set-034. Mitigation for re-implementation cost: git history at v0.18.x preserves the full implementation; future re-enable fetches from history rather than from a frozen-and-rotting source tree (same pattern as Set 036 Session 3's codex config-toml watcher retirement). Cross-provider consensus deferred per [[feedback_ai_router_usage]] mid-session restriction; the disposition is mechanical rather than judgment-laden once the headline finding surfaced. Operator confirmed via AskUserQuestion: 'Delete orphan source (Recommended)'.

Layer-3 Playwright sweep — 4 pre-existing failures all addressed (baseline was 21 passed / 2 skipped / 4 failed; post-cleanup is 24 passed / 2 skipped / 0 failed). Round A verification routed via task_type='verification' (tier-routed, NOT session-verification which is pinned to gpt-5-4) per [[feedback_split_large_verification_bundles]] precedent + the Set 036 S5 429-cascade fallback that landed on opus at $1.25 — the cleanup is documentation-heavy and the cheaper tiers should suffice. Router selected gemini-pro (complexity_score=57, tier 2); 22.4s elapsed; $0.03835 (27576 input + 388 output tokens). Bundle: ui-audit-findings.md + full git diff excluding generated artifacts (90,351 chars total). All six verification asks returned PASS: (1) audit completeness — the audit was thorough, correctly identified the Claude-specific UI as already non-functional, and remaining engine-specific copy is correctly scoped; (2) Recommendation interface move — clean, tsc --noEmit confirms no broken callers; (3) orphan-CSS deletion bounds — self-contained block; tree shell styles unaffected; (4) broken-test sweep completeness — 4-to-0 failure reduction confirms the sweep landed cleanly; (5) comment freshness — all affected files diligently updated; (6) out-of-scope check — orphan cleanup correctly scoped; the bucket-count regex fix is an acceptable opportunistic correction within a file being modified, properly documented. Round B not warranted (no Major / Blocker / Minor findings).
